title: Formal Tone — Lift Casual Phrasing into Clinical Register
brief: Each sentence below is written in casual or conversational English. Rewrite each using a professional clinical register suitable for an OET letter.

items.informal: The patient is doing okay-ish lately and his sugars are not too bad.
acceptableFormal: blood glucose has been adequately controlled
items.exemplar: The patient has remained clinically stable, and his blood glucose has been adequately controlled.
items.rationale: Replace colloquial hedges ('okay-ish', 'not too bad') with neutral clinical phrasing.

items.informal: He's been feeling pretty rough since the op and can't really get out of bed much.
items.exemplar: Since surgery his recovery has been slow, and he reports limited mobility with significant fatigue.
items.rationale: Avoid contractions and vague intensifiers; use precise clinical vocabulary.

items.informal: Could you guys have a look and let us know what's going on?
items.exemplar: I would appreciate your assessment and any further recommendations regarding ongoing management.
items.rationale: Closure must be polite and professional; address the recipient as a single specialist, not a group.
